perturbation.original_text: 5.01 TERM. The term ("Term") of this Agreement shall be for one hundred eighty days (180) from the date set forth below unless Network 1 or Visa or MasterCard or Harris Bank doesn't approve Affiliate's ISO application, in which case, the Term will be 3 years. This Agreement will automatically renew for successive one-year terms unless terminated by either party by providing the other with 30 days written notice that this Agreement will not be renewed or Affiliate enters into a Processing agreement with Network 1 and an ISO Sponsorship agreement with Harris Bank in which case this Agreement will automatically terminate concurrent with the execution of such agreements.
perturbation.changed_text: 5.01 TERM. The term ("Term") of this Agreement shall be for one hundred eighty days (180) from the date set forth below unless Network 1 or Visa or MasterCard or Harris Bank doesn't approve Affiliate's ISO application, in which case, the Term will be 3 years. This Agreement will automatically renew for successive one-year terms unless terminated by either party by providing the other with 30 days written notice that this Agreement will not be renewed. This Agreement can only be terminated after the initial 180 days if Affiliate enters into a Processing agreement with Network 1 and an ISO Sponsorship agreement with Harris Bank in which case this Agreement will automatically terminate concurrent with the execution of such agreements.
perturbation.explanation: This change introduces an in-text contradiction by stating the agreement can 'only be terminated after the initial 180 days if Affiliate enters into a Processing agreement', conflicting with the previous statement that 'either party [can terminate] by providing the other with 30 days written notice'. This contradiction creates uncertainty about the conditions under which the agreement can be terminated after the initial 180 days and before an ISO agreement is executed.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 5.01 TERM. The term ("Term") of this Agreement shall be for one hundred eighty days (180) from the date set forth below unless Network 1 or Visa or MasterCard or Harris Bank doesn't approve Affiliate's ISO application, in which case, the Term will be 3 years. This Agreement will automatically renew for successive one-year terms unless terminated by either party by providing the other with 30 days written notice that this Agreement will not be renewed. This Agreement can only be terminated after the initial 180 days if Affiliate enters into a Processing agreement with Network 1 and an ISO Sponsorship agreement with Harris Bank in which case this Agreement will automatically terminate concurrent with the execution of such agreements.
perturbation.justification: The changed text introduces a direct contradiction. The original text allows either party to terminate the agreement with 30 days' notice, while the changed text restricts termination to only when the Affiliate enters into specific processing and sponsorship agreements. This creates an inconsistency regarding the conditions under which termination is permissible, satisfying the definition of "Inconsistencies - In Text Contradiction."

perturbation.original_text: 5.03 EFFECT OF TERMINATION. Upon termination of this Agreement, except in the event of termination due to an uncured default by Affiliate pursuant to Section 5.02 [TERMINATION]A, Network 1 shall continue to pay to the Affiliate the Affiliate's Fee described in Section 3.01 [AMOUNT]B for so long as Network 1 continues to process transactions of the Merchants in accordance with Schedule B attached hereto and Affiliate continues to maintain the merchant accounts, provided, however, that no such Affiliate's Fees shall be earned or paid for any calendar month in which the Affiliate's Fee does not exceed $500. Upon termination due to an uncured default by Affiliate, or termination for cause pursuant to Section 5.02 [TERMINATION]D all Affiliates fees shall cease to be accrued and paid immediately upon the occurrence of said event. Additionally, upon termination of this contract for any reason, all merchants recruited by Affiliate on behalf of Network 1 for any product offered through Network 1, Affiliate shall not approach, rewrite, pursue, or contract with any current client for the purpose of obtaining said client as a new customer for Affiliate or any competing entity the Affiliate may be in contract with. Such restriction shall also apply to Affiliate's past, current and future officers, directors, sales representatives, and Contractors. If terminated for cause Affiliate is responsible for any expense incurred by Network 1 for investigating incidents, attorney fees, fines, and administration expense.
perturbation.changed_text: 5.03 EFFECT OF TERMINATION. Upon termination of this Agreement, except in the event of termination due to an uncured default by Affiliate pursuant to Section 5.02 [TERMINATION]A, Network 1 shall continue to pay to the Affiliate the Affiliate's Fee described in Section 3.01 [AMOUNT]B for so long as Network 1 continues to process transactions of the Merchants in accordance with Schedule B attached hereto and Affiliate continues to maintain the merchant accounts, provided, however, that no such Affiliate's Fees shall be earned or paid for any calendar month in which the Affiliate's Fee does not exceed $500. Upon termination due to an uncured default by Affiliate, or termination for cause pursuant to Section 5.02 [TERMINATION]D all Affiliates fees shall cease to be accrued and paid immediately upon the occurrence of said event. Additionally, upon termination of this contract for any reason, all merchants recruited by Affiliate on behalf of Network 1 for any product offered through Network 1, Affiliate may approach, rewrite, pursue, or contract with any current client for the purpose of obtaining said client as a new customer for Affiliate or any competing entity the Affiliate may be in contract with. Such restriction shall also apply to Affiliate's past, current and future officers, directors, sales representatives, and Contractors. If terminated for cause Affiliate is responsible for any expense incurred by Network 1 for investigating incidents, attorney fees, fines, and administration expense.
perturbation.explanation: This change creates an in-text contradiction regarding post-termination client interaction. The original text restricts the Affiliate from contacting Network 1's clients after termination. By removing 'not' from 'shall not approach', the modified text now states the opposite - that the Affiliate *may* contact those clients. This creates a direct conflict in the contractual obligations, making it unclear what the Affiliate is allowed to do after the agreement ends. This creates uncertainty on enforcement, due to the in-text contradiction.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 5.03 EFFECT OF TERMINATION. Upon termination of this Agreement, except in the event of termination due to an uncured default by Affiliate pursuant to Section 5.02 [TERMINATION]A, Network 1 shall continue to pay to the Affiliate the Affiliate's Fee described in Section 3.01 [AMOUNT]B for so long as Network 1 continues to process transactions of the Merchants in accordance with Schedule B attached hereto and Affiliate continues to maintain the merchant accounts, provided, however, that no such Affiliate's Fees shall be earned or paid for any calendar month in which the Affiliate's Fee does not exceed $500. Upon termination due to an uncured default by Affiliate, or termination for cause pursuant to Section 5.02 [TERMINATION]D all Affiliates fees shall cease to be accrued and paid immediately upon the occurrence of said event. Additionally, upon termination of this contract for any reason, all merchants recruited by Affiliate on behalf of Network 1 for any product offered through Network 1, Affiliate may approach, rewrite, pursue, or contract with any current client for the purpose of obtaining said client as a new customer for Affiliate or any competing entity the Affiliate may be in contract with. Such restriction shall also apply to Affiliate's past, current and future officers, directors, sales representatives, and Contractors. If terminated for cause Affiliate is responsible for any expense incurred by Network 1 for investigating incidents, attorney fees, fines, and administration expense.
perturbation.justification: The changed text removes the prohibition on the Affiliate contacting Network 1's clients after termination, directly contradicting the original intent of the clause and creating an inconsistency regarding post-termination obligations. The original text states the affiliate 'shall not approach', whereas the changed text states the affiliate 'may approach'. This creates a direct conflict within section 5.03 as referenced by the 'Contradicted Text' section, because the clause now says the opposite of what it initially said, and both are in the same section. Therefore, a strong in-text contradiction is introduced by the change.
